description: This figure characterizes the phase composition and microstructure of as-cast and hot forged high-Al (~5 wt%) low-density medium Mn steel (Fe-7.79Mn-4.88Al-0.84Si-0.2C). Subplot (a) presents XRD patterns (2θ 40–120°) for cast (black line) and hot forged (red line) specimens, confirming both contain BCC δ-ferrite and FCC austenite; Rietveld refinement yields ~26 vol% austenite in cast samples and ~29 vol% in forged samples. Subplots (b,c) are optical micrographs of cast specimens, showing dendritic austenite (marked by blue arrows) in a δ-ferrite matrix. Subplots (d,e) are optical micrographs of hot forged specimens, with residual dendritic austenite (blue arrow) remaining due to insufficient pre-forging soaking. Subplot (f) is an SEM micrograph of the forged specimen showing banded δ/γ morphology, while subplot (g) is an EBSD phase map (red: BCC ferrite, green: FCC austenite) validating phase distribution. The figure demonstrates hot forging marginally increases austenite fraction while partially dissolving cast dendritic microstructure, supporting that elemental partitioning during soaking improves austenite stability.
